revision.text:
Princess Nakatsu, also known as Nakatsu-hime no Mikoto, is a figure in Japanese mythology and the consort of [[Emperor Ōjin]] and the mother of [[Emperor Nintoku]].<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |last=文也 |date=2019-05-26 |title=仲姫命とはどんな人？ |url=https://rekishi.sseikatsu.net/nakahime/ |access-date=2023-01-19 |website=歴史好きブログ |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=朝日日本歴史人物事典,デジタル版 |title=仲姫命(なかつひめのみこと)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BB%B2%E5%A7%AB%E5%91%BD-1096807 |access-date=2023-01-19 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref>
There are few known episodes of Princess Nakatsu's life, but she is venerated as a goddess at [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]].<ref name=":0" />
Princess Nakatsu was the daughter of Hondamawaka no Ō (also known as Hondamawaka no Mikoto), a descendant of the emperor Kōshō and her mother was Kana-taya hime. Princess Nakatsu was the sister of Takano-iri hime and Otohime. She married Ōjin and had two children, Arata-hime no Mikoto and Emperor Nintoku.<ref name=":0" />
Princess Nakatsu is believed to have become empress consort to Emperor Ōjin in the 2nd year of his reign<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=朝日日本歴史人物事典,デジタル版 |title=仲姫命(なかつひめのみこと)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BB%B2%E5%A7%AB%E5%91%BD-1096807 |access-date=2023-01-19 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref><ref name=":0" />. According to the Nihon Shoki, her sisters Takano-iri hime and Otohime also became consorts of Emperor Ōjin<ref name=":1" />. This was a common practice during this period for sisters to be consorts of the emperor.<ref name=":0" />
After the death of Emperor Ōjin, Princess Nakatsu became empress dowager and the mother of the new emperor, Emperor Nintoku.<ref name=":0" />
The tomb of Princess Nakatsu is believed to be located in Nakatsu-yama no Misasagi, Nara prefecture.<ref name=":0" />
== References ==

Princess Nakatsu, also known as Nakatsu-hime no Mikoto, is a figure in Japanese mythology and the consort of [[Emperor Ōjin]] and the mother of [[Emperor Nintoku]].<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |last=文也 |date=2019-05-26 |title=仲姫命とはどんな人？ |url=https://rekishi.sseikatsu.net/nakahime/ |access-date=2023-01-19 |website=歴史好きブログ |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=朝日日本歴史人物事典,デジタル版 |title=仲姫命(なかつひめのみこと)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BB%B2%E5%A7%AB%E5%91%BD-1096807 |access-date=2023-01-19 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref>
There are few known episodes of Princess Nakatsu's life, but she is venerated as a goddess at [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]].<ref name=":0" />
Princess Nakatsu was the daughter of Hondamawaka no Ō (also known as Hondamawaka no Mikoto), a descendant of the emperor Kōshō and her mother was Kana-taya hime. Princess Nakatsu was the sister of Takano-iri hime and Otohime. She married Ōjin and had two children, Arata-hime no Mikoto and Emperor Nintoku.<ref name=":0" />
Princess Nakatsu is believed to have become empress consort to Emperor Ōjin in the 2nd year of his reign<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=朝日日本歴史人物事典,デジタル版 |title=仲姫命(なかつひめのみこと)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BB%B2%E5%A7%AB%E5%91%BD-1096807 |access-date=2023-01-19 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref><ref name=":0" />. According to the Nihon Shoki, her sisters Takano-iri hime and Otohime also became consorts of Emperor Ōjin<ref name=":1" />. This was a common practice during this period for sisters to be consorts of the emperor.<ref name=":0" />
After the death of Emperor Ōjin, Princess Nakatsu became empress dowager and the mother of the new emperor, Emperor Nintoku.<ref name=":0" />
The tomb of Princess Nakatsu is believed to be located in Nakatsu-yama no Misasagi, Nara prefecture.<ref name=":0" />

The tomb of Princess Nakatsu is believed to be located in Nakatsu-yama no Misasagi, Nara prefecture.<ref name=":0" />

{{Infobox person|image=File:Nakatsuhime Yakushiji.jpg|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Ōjin|qid=Q317997}}|children={{ill|Nintoku|qid=Q313119}}|father={{ill|Q14759204|qid=Q14759204}}|mother={{ill|Q110931057|qid=Q110931057}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11378496]]
Wikidata name: Nakatsuhime no Mikoto
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Nakatsuhime Yakushiji.jpg|thumb|Nakatsuhime Yakushiji]]
'''Princess Nakatsu''', also known as '''Nakatsu-hime no Mikoto''', is a figure in [[Japanese mythology]] who was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and the mother of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].<ref name="sseikatsu">{{Cite web |last=文也 |date=2019-05-26 |title=仲姫命とはどんな人？ |url=https://rekishi.sseikatsu.net/nakahime/ |access-date=2023-01-19 |website=歴史好きブログ |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="日本人名大辞典+plus-2023a" />

==History==
There are few known episodes of Princess Nakatsu's life, but she is venerated as a goddess at [[Hachiman shrine]]s.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

Princess Nakatsu was the daughter of Hondamawaka no Ō (also known as Hondamawaka no Mikoto), a descendant of [[:en:Emperor Kōshō|Emperor Kōshō]] and her mother was Kana-taya hime. Princess Nakatsu was the sister of Takano-iri hime and Otohime. She married Ōjin and had two children, Arata-hime no Mikoto and Emperor Nintoku.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

Princess Nakatsu is believed to have become empress consort to Emperor Ōjin in the 2nd year of his reign.<ref name="日本人名大辞典+plus-2023a">{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=朝日日本歴史人物事典,デジタル版 |title=仲姫命(なかつひめのみこと)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BB%B2%E5%A7%AB%E5%91%BD-1096807 |access-date=2023-01-19 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="sseikatsu"/> According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', her sisters Takano-iri hime and Otohime also became consorts of Emperor Ōjin.<ref name="日本人名大辞典+plus-2023a" /> This was a common practice during this period for sisters to be consorts of the emperor.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

After the death of Emperor Ōjin, Princess Nakatsu became empress dowager and the mother of the new emperor, Emperor Nintoku.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

The tomb of Princess Nakatsu is believed to be located at Nakatsu-yama no Misasagi in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

{{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=}} Is a kofun in [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]]. It is said that Nakatsuhime was buried there.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Nakatsuhime-no-mikoto-ryo Kofun{{!}}藤井寺市 |url=https://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/kanko/language/english/furuichimoundedtombgroup/1473315515279.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |website=www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp |language=ja}}</ref>

==Family tree==
{{Generations of Jimmu}}

==Notes==
{{notelist}}

==References==
{{Reflist}}

{{s-end}}

It is said that Nakatsuhime was buried there.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Nakatsuhime-no-mikoto-ryo Kofun{{!}}藤井寺市 |url=https://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/kanko/language/english/furuichimoundedtombgroup/1473315515279.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |website=www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp |language=ja}}</ref>

It is said that Nakatsuhime was buried there.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Nakatsuhime-no-mikoto-ryo Kofun{{!}}藤井寺市 |url=https://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/kanko/language/english/furuichimoundedtombgroup/1473315515279.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |website=www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp |language=ja}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Empress Nakatsuhime]][[en:Nakatsuhime]][[fa:ناکاتسو هیمه]][[ja:仲姫命]][[zh:仲姬命]]
{{Infobox person|image=File:Nakatsuhime Yakushiji.jpg|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}}|children={{ill|Nintoku|WD=Q313119}}|father={{ill|Q14759204|WD=Q14759204}}|mother={{ill|Q110931057|WD=Q110931057}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11378496]]
Wikidata name: Nakatsuhime no Mikoto
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Nakatsuhime Yakushiji.jpg|thumb|Nakatsuhime Yakushiji]]
'''Princess Nakatsu''', also known as '''Nakatsu-hime no Mikoto''', is a figure in [[Japanese mythology]] who was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and the mother of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].<ref name="sseikatsu">{{Cite web |last=文也 |date=2019-05-26 |title=仲姫命とはどんな人？ |url=https://rekishi.sseikatsu.net/nakahime/ |access-date=2023-01-19 |website=歴史好きブログ |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="日本人名大辞典+plus-2023a" />

==History==
There are few known episodes of Princess Nakatsu's life, but she is venerated as a goddess at [[Hachiman shrine]]s.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

Princess Nakatsu was the daughter of Hondamawaka no Ō (also known as Hondamawaka no Mikoto), a descendant of [[:en:Emperor Kōshō|Emperor Kōshō]] and her mother was Kana-taya hime. Princess Nakatsu was the sister of Takano-iri hime and Otohime. She married Ōjin and had two children, Arata-hime no Mikoto and Emperor Nintoku.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

Princess Nakatsu is believed to have become empress consort to Emperor Ōjin in the 2nd year of his reign.<ref name="日本人名大辞典+plus-2023a">{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=朝日日本歴史人物事典,デジタル版 |title=仲姫命(なかつひめのみこと)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BB%B2%E5%A7%AB%E5%91%BD-1096807 |access-date=2023-01-19 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="sseikatsu"/> According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', her sisters Takano-iri hime and Otohime also became consorts of Emperor Ōjin.<ref name="日本人名大辞典+plus-2023a" /> This was a common practice during this period for sisters to be consorts of the emperor.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

After the death of Emperor Ōjin, Princess Nakatsu became empress dowager and the mother of the new emperor, Emperor Nintoku.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

The tomb of Princess Nakatsu is believed to be located at Nakatsu-yama no Misasagi in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

{{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=}} Is a kofun in [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]]. It is said that Nakatsuhime was buried there.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Nakatsuhime-no-mikoto-ryo Kofun{{!}}藤井寺市 |url=https://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/kanko/language/english/furuichimoundedtombgroup/1473315515279.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |website=www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp |language=ja}}</ref>

It is said that Nakatsuhime was buried there.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Nakatsuhime-no-mikoto-ryo Kofun{{!}}藤井寺市 |url=https://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/kanko/language/english/furuichimoundedtombgroup/1473315515279.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |website=www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp |language=ja}}</ref>

{{Infobox person|image=File:Nakatsuhime Yakushiji.jpg|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}}|children={{ill|Nintoku|WD=Q313119}}|father={{ill|Q14759204|WD=Q14759204}}|mother={{ill|Q110931057|WD=Q110931057}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11378496]]

[[File:Nakatsuhime Yakushiji.jpg|thumb|Nakatsuhime Yakushiji]]
'''Princess Nakatsu''', also known as '''Nakatsu-hime no Mikoto''', is a figure in [[Japanese mythology]] who was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and the mother of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].<ref name="sseikatsu">{{Cite web |last=文也 |date=2019-05-26 |title=仲姫命とはどんな人？ |url=https://rekishi.sseikatsu.net/nakahime/ |access-date=2023-01-19 |website=歴史好きブログ |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="日本人名大辞典+plus-2023a" />

==History==
There are few known episodes of Princess Nakatsu's life, but she is venerated as a goddess at [[Hachiman shrine]]s.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

Princess Nakatsu was the daughter of Hondamawaka no Ō (also known as Hondamawaka no Mikoto), a descendant of [[:en:Emperor Kōshō|Emperor Kōshō]] and her mother was Kana-taya hime. Princess Nakatsu was the sister of Takano-iri hime and Otohime. She married Ōjin and had two children, Arata-hime no Mikoto and Emperor Nintoku.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

Princess Nakatsu is believed to have become empress consort to Emperor Ōjin in the 2nd year of his reign.<ref name="日本人名大辞典+plus-2023a">{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=朝日日本歴史人物事典,デジタル版 |title=仲姫命(なかつひめのみこと)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BB%B2%E5%A7%AB%E5%91%BD-1096807 |access-date=2023-01-19 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="sseikatsu"/> According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', her sisters Takano-iri hime and Otohime also became consorts of Emperor Ōjin.<ref name="日本人名大辞典+plus-2023a" /> This was a common practice during this period for sisters to be consorts of the emperor.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

After the death of Emperor Ōjin, Princess Nakatsu became empress dowager and the mother of the new emperor, Emperor Nintoku.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

The tomb of Princess Nakatsu is believed to be located at Nakatsu-yama no Misasagi in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

{{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=}} Is a kofun in [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]]. It is said that Nakatsuhime was buried there.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Nakatsuhime-no-mikoto-ryo Kofun{{!}}藤井寺市 |url=https://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/kanko/language/english/furuichimoundedtombgroup/1473315515279.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |website=www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp |language=ja}}</ref>

Princess Nakatsu was the daughter of Hondamawaka no Ō (also known as Hondamawaka no Mikoto), a descendant of [[:en:Emperor Kōshō|Emperor Kōshō]] and her mother was Kana-taya hime. Princess Nakatsu was the sister of Takano-iri hime and Otohime. She married Ōjin and had two children, Arata-hime no Mikoto and Emperor Nintoku.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

Princess Nakatsu is believed to have become empress consort to Emperor Ōjin in the 2nd year of his reign.<ref name="日本人名大辞典+plus-2023a">{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=朝日日本歴史人物事典,デジタル版 |title=仲姫命(なかつひめのみこと)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BB%B2%E5%A7%AB%E5%91%BD-1096807 |access-date=2023-01-19 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="sseikatsu"/> According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', her sisters Takano-iri hime and Otohime also became consorts of Emperor Ōjin.<ref name="日本人名大辞典+plus-2023a" /> This was a common practice during this period for sisters to be consorts of the emperor.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

After the death of Emperor Ōjin, Princess Nakatsu became empress dowager and the mother of the new emperor, Emperor Nintoku.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

The tomb of Princess Nakatsu is believed to be located at Nakatsu-yama no Misasagi in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

{{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=}} Is a kofun in [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]]. It is said that Nakatsuhime was buried there.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Nakatsuhime-no-mikoto-ryo Kofun{{!}}藤井寺市 |url=https://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/kanko/language/english/furuichimoundedtombgroup/1473315515279.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |website=www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp |language=ja}}</ref>

==History==
There are few known episodes of Princess Nakatsu's life, but she is venerated as a goddess at [[Hachiman shrine]]s.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

Princess Nakatsu was the daughter of Hondamawaka no Ō (also known as Hondamawaka no Mikoto), a descendant of [[:en:Emperor Kōshō|Emperor Kōshō]] and her mother was Kana-taya hime. The tomb of Princess Nakatsu is believed to be located at Nakatsu-yama no Misasagi in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Nakatsuhime Yakushiji.jpg|thumb|Nakatsuhime Yakushiji]]
'''Princess Nakatsu''', also known as '''Nakatsu-hime no Mikoto''', is a figure in [[Japanese mythology]] who was the consort of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} and the mother of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}.<ref name="sseikatsu">{{Cite web |last=文也 |date=2019-05-26 |title=仲姫命とはどんな人？ |url=https://rekishi.sseikatsu.net/nakahime/ |access-date=2023-01-19 |website=歴史好きブログ |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="日本人名大辞典+plus-2023a" />

==History==
There are few known episodes of Princess Nakatsu's life, but she is venerated as a goddess at [[Hachiman shrine]]s.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

Princess Nakatsu was the daughter of Hondamawaka no Ō (also known as Hondamawaka no Mikoto), a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō}} and her mother was Kana-taya hime. Princess Nakatsu was the sister of Takano-iri hime and Otohime. She married Ōjin and had two children, Arata-hime no Mikoto and Emperor Nintoku.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

Princess Nakatsu is believed to have become empress consort to Emperor Ōjin in the 2nd year of his reign.<ref name="日本人名大辞典+plus-2023a">{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=朝日日本歴史人物事典,デジタル版 |title=仲姫命(なかつひめのみこと)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BB%B2%E5%A7%AB%E5%91%BD-1096807 |access-date=2023-01-19 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="sseikatsu"/> According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', her sisters Takano-iri hime and Otohime also became consorts of Emperor Ōjin.<ref name="日本人名大辞典+plus-2023a" /> This was a common practice during this period for sisters to be consorts of the emperor.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

After the death of Emperor Ōjin, Princess Nakatsu became empress dowager and the mother of the new emperor, Emperor Nintoku.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

The tomb of Princess Nakatsu is believed to be located at Nakatsu-yama no Misasagi in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

{{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=}} Is a kofun in {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujiidera}}. It is said that Nakatsuhime was buried there.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Nakatsuhime-no-mikoto-ryo Kofun{{!}}藤井寺市 |url=https://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/kanko/language/english/furuichimoundedtombgroup/1473315515279.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |website=www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp |language=ja}}</ref>

Princess Nakatsu was the sister of Takano-iri hime and Otohime. She married Ōjin and had two children, Arata-hime no Mikoto and Emperor Nintoku.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

Princess Nakatsu is believed to have become empress consort to Emperor Ōjin in the 2nd year of his reign.<ref name="日本人名大辞典+plus-2023a">{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=朝日日本歴史人物事典,デジタル版 |title=仲姫命(なかつひめのみこと)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BB%B2%E5%A7%AB%E5%91%BD-1096807 |access-date=2023-01-19 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="sseikatsu"/> According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', her sisters Takano-iri hime and Otohime also became consorts of Emperor Ōjin.<ref name="日本人名大辞典+plus-2023a" /> This was a common practice during this period for sisters to be consorts of the emperor.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

After the death of Emperor Ōjin, Princess Nakatsu became empress dowager and the mother of the new emperor, Emperor Nintoku.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

The tomb of Princess Nakatsu is believed to be located at Nakatsu-yama no Misasagi in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

{{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=}} Is a kofun in {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujiidera}}. It is said that Nakatsuhime was buried there.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Nakatsuhime-no-mikoto-ryo Kofun{{!}}藤井寺市 |url=https://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/kanko/language/english/furuichimoundedtombgroup/1473315515279.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |website=www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp |language=ja}}</ref>

There are few known episodes of Princess Nakatsu's life, but she is venerated as a goddess at [[Hachiman shrine]]s.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

After the death of Emperor Ōjin, Princess Nakatsu became empress dowager and the mother of the new emperor, Emperor Nintoku.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

It is said that Nakatsuhime was buried there.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Nakatsuhime-no-mikoto-ryo Kofun{{!}}藤井寺市 |url=https://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/kanko/language/english/furuichimoundedtombgroup/1473315515279.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |website=www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp |language=ja}}</ref>

[[File:Nakatsuhime Yakushiji.jpg|thumb|Nakatsuhime Yakushiji]]
'''Princess Nakatsu''', also known as '''Nakatsu-hime no Mikoto''', is a figure in [[Japanese mythology]] who was the consort of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} and the mother of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}.<ref name="sseikatsu">{{Cite web |last=文也 |date=2019-05-26 |title=仲姫命とはどんな人？ |url=https://rekishi.sseikatsu.net/nakahime/ |access-date=2023-01-19 |website=歴史好きブログ |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="日本人名大辞典+plus-2023a" />

==History==
There are few known episodes of Princess Nakatsu's life, but she is venerated as a goddess at [[Hachiman shrine]]s.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

Princess Nakatsu was the daughter of Hondamawaka no Ō (also known as Hondamawaka no Mikoto), a descendant of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō|1=Emperor Kōshō}} and her mother was Kana-taya hime. Princess Nakatsu was the sister of Takano-iri hime and Otohime. She married Ōjin and had two children, Arata-hime no Mikoto and Emperor Nintoku.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

Princess Nakatsu is believed to have become empress consort to Emperor Ōjin in the 2nd year of his reign.<ref name="日本人名大辞典+plus-2023a">{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=朝日日本歴史人物事典,デジタル版 |title=仲姫命(なかつひめのみこと)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BB%B2%E5%A7%AB%E5%91%BD-1096807 |access-date=2023-01-19 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="sseikatsu"/> According to the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', her sisters Takano-iri hime and Otohime also became consorts of Emperor Ōjin.<ref name="日本人名大辞典+plus-2023a" /> This was a common practice during this period for sisters to be consorts of the emperor.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

After the death of Emperor Ōjin, Princess Nakatsu became empress dowager and the mother of the new emperor, Emperor Nintoku.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

The tomb of Princess Nakatsu is believed to be located at Nakatsu-yama no Misasagi in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}.<ref name="sseikatsu"/>

{{ill|Nakatsuyama Kofun|ja|仲ツ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsuyama Kofun|qq=}} Is a kofun in {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujiidera|1=Fujiidera}}. It is said that Nakatsuhime was buried there.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Nakatsuhime-no-mikoto-ryo Kofun{{!}}藤井寺市 |url=https://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/kanko/language/english/furuichimoundedtombgroup/1473315515279.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |website=www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp |language=ja}}</ref>
